goal: Quick Pull (Ask-a-Question single card) poster rendered Thai frame but an English reading paragraph
root_cause: The poster builds strings from payload.lang, but ensureTarotData()->loadTarotData() (no arg) infers the deck slice from the PAGE URL (currentLangForData: /th/ or ?l=), not the poster language. A TH poster generated on a non-/th/ page loaded the EN-only cards slice, so every getLocalizedField(card,'<field>','th') (love_present_th, reading_summary_present_th, standalone_present_th, general_meaning_th) missed and resolveQuestionPosterSummaries fell through to the EN gap-fillers (standalone_present_en) — English text on a Thai poster, and the generic standalone instead of the topic-specific love reading.
fix: ensureTarotData(lang) now loads the full bilingual deck ('both' = data/cards.json) whenever the poster's locale fields aren't present in the cached deck (deckHasLocale check); buildPoster + buildCardEntries pass payload.lang. EN posters with the EN slice already loaded stay light; only a cross-URL TH poster upgrades to 'both'.
verified: Rendered the screenshot's card (39-three-of-cups reversed, topic=love) in chrome harness: lang=th now shows love_present_th ('ระวังเพื่อนรักหักเหลี่ยม...'), lang=en shows love_present_en ('Beware of a friend betraying you...'); badge orientation (ไพ่กลับหัว/Reversed) consistent; card name stays EN by design. md5 parity canonical=www=ios.
